_meta.description: Soutien au travail autonome (STA) — Quebec government income support program for people starting a self-employed business. Provides weekly income replacement while you build your business.

program_name_en: Soutien au travail autonome (STA) — Self-Employment Income Support
plain_language_summary: ⚠️ PROGRAMME SUSPENDU — The STA program has been suspended since May 1, 2024 for an indefinite period. No new applications are being accepted. The information below is kept for reference in case the program resumes. Do NOT present this as an available option to users.
suspension_note: Suspended indefinitely as of May 1, 2024. No new applications accepted. Check Emploi-Québec for updates on whether the program has resumed.

amount.note: Program is currently suspended — these amounts are historical reference only.

plain_language_how_it_works: When active: unemployed individuals who want to start a business could collect STA instead of EI while building their business. Emploi-Québec would assign an advisor to follow progress monthly. Currently suspended as of May 1, 2024.

eligible: Recently unemployed and EI-eligible (check with Emploi-Québec)
Recipients of certain social assistance programs
Some other qualifying circumstances — verify with Emploi-Québec
employment_status.note: This is the main eligibility gate — you generally need to be EI-eligible or currently receiving EI
employment_status.important: If you're currently employed full-time, you don't qualify. The program is for people in job transition.

immigration_status.note: Work permit holders are generally not eligible as they may not qualify for EI. Verify with Emploi-Québec for your specific situation.
immigration_status.future_eligibility: Permanent residents qualify. If you're transitioning from work permit to PR, you may qualify shortly after receiving PR status.

what_they_assess: Your business idea has genuine commercial viability
You have relevant skills or experience
You're committed to full-time business development
business_viability.how_assessed: Through an initial evaluation meeting with an Emploi-Québec advisor

age.maximum: No age cap — available at any age
age.scoring: Available to all ages — no age penalty

sector.note: Home daycares (RSG) are specifically mentioned as eligible businesses

what_you_must_do_to_maintain_support: Work full-time on your business (no other employment during program)
Meet monthly with your Emploi-Québec advisor
Complete the business plan and follow through on milestones
Register your business (NEQ) and comply with applicable regulations

process.title: Contact your local Emploi-Québec / Services Québec office
process.detail: Express interest in the STA program. You can also be referred by PME MTL, YES Montréal, or other partner organizations.

process.title: Initial eligibility assessment
process.detail: An advisor evaluates your situation, your business idea, and your EI/employment status.

process.title: Prepare your business plan
process.detail: You'll need a business plan to show your business is viable. Emploi-Québec advisors and partner organizations (PME MTL, YES Montréal) can help you prepare this.

process.title: Application review and approval
process.detail: Emploi-Québec reviews your plan and approves participation. This can take 2-6 weeks.

process.title: Program start — monthly payments and advisor meetings
process.detail: Payments begin and you meet monthly with your advisor to report progress and get support.

prerequisites.step: Have a business idea ready to develop
prerequisites.note: The earlier you apply after job loss, the more EI entitlement you can convert to STA

partner_organizations.name: Emploi-Québec / Services Québec
partner_organizations.note: Direct entry point for STA — book appointment at local office

partner_organizations.name: YES Montréal
partner_organizations.note: Can refer you to STA and help prepare your business plan for the application

partner_organizations.name: PME MTL
partner_organizations.note: Local offices can facilitate STA referrals and help with business plan

persona_fit.Yara: Potentially strong fit if unemployed or recently left previous job. Syrian immigrant — must be permanent resident to qualify.
persona_fit.Fatima: Very strong fit — single mother starting home daycare, likely transitioning from employment or social assistance. RSG startup is specifically mentioned as eligible.
persona_fit.Marcus: Less likely fit — McGill graduate likely not EI-eligible as student

common_mistakes: Not knowing this program exists — it's underutilized and underadvertised
Waiting too long after job loss to apply — EI entitlement that hasn't been used can often be converted to STA
Thinking work permit holders automatically qualify — immigration status matters here
Not taking the monthly advisor meetings seriously — they're required and can actually be very helpful
Starting to earn significant business revenue without reporting it — this affects your STA payments
government_body: Emploi-Québec (Ministère du Travail, de l'Emploi et de la Solidarité sociale)
